task.title: Fix File Permission Issues
task.description: Resolve permission errors preventing the application from reading log files.
constraints: DO NOT use chmod 777 or chmod 666
Set minimal required permissions (e.g., 644 for files, 755 for directories)
Only modify permissions for logs/ directory

steps.thought: Let me check the current permissions on the logs directory.
steps.observation:
total 48
drwx------ 2 root root  4096 Dec 19 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 user user  4096 Dec 19 09:00 ..
-rw------- 1 root root 15234 Dec 19 16:45 app.log
-rw------- 1 root root  8192 Dec 19 16:30 error.log
-rw------- 1 root root  4096 Dec 19 16:15 access.log

steps.thought: The logs are owned by root with 600 permissions. Let me check what user the app runs as.
App runs as 'appuser'. According to constraints, I must use minimal permissions. I'll set 644 for files and 755 for directories.
Now let me set the log files to 644 (owner can write, others can read).

steps.observation:
total 48
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root  4096 Dec 19 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 user user  4096 Dec 19 09:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 15234 Dec 19 16:45 app.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  8192 Dec 19 16:30 error.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  4096 Dec 19 16:15 access.log

steps.thought: Good! Directory is 755, files are 644. But the files are still owned by root. Let me change ownership to appuser.
Let me verify ownership and permissions.
steps.observation:
total 48
drwxr-xr-x 2 appuser appuser  4096 Dec 19 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 user    user     4096 Dec 19 09:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 appuser appuser 15234 Dec 19 16:45 app.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 appuser appuser  8192 Dec 19 16:30 error.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 appuser appuser  4096 Dec 19 16:15 access.log

steps.thought: Perfect! Now appuser owns the files with 644 permissions. Let me test if the application can read logs.
steps.observation:
[2024-12-19 16:45:23] INFO: Server started on port 3000
[2024-12-19 16:45:24] INFO: Database connection established
[2024-12-19 16:45:25] INFO: Routes initialized
[2024-12-19 16:45:30] INFO: First request received
[2024-12-19 16:45:31] INFO: Processing user authentication

steps.thought: Application can read logs! Let me test write access.

arguments.command: sudo -u appuser sh -c 'echo "[2024-12-19 17:25:00] INFO: Permission test" >> logs/app.log'

steps.thought: Let me verify the write succeeded.
Perfect! Read and write both work. Let me restart the service.
Let me verify the service started successfully.
steps.observation:
● app-server.service - Application Server
   Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/app-server.service; enabled)
   Active: active (running) since Thu 2024-12-19 17:26:05 UTC; 2s ago
 Main PID: 23456 (node)
    Tasks: 11 (limit: 4915)
   Memory: 45.2M
   CGroup: /system.slice/app-server.service
           └─23456 node server.js

steps.thought: Service running! Let me test the health endpoint.
Let me verify the final permissions follow best practices.
steps.observation:
644 appuser:appuser logs/access.log
644 appuser:appuser logs/app.log
644 appuser:appuser logs/error.log

steps.thought: All permissions set correctly following security best practices.

arguments.message: Successfully fixed log file permissions using minimal required permissions: 755 for directory, 644 for files. Ownership changed to appuser. Application can read and write logs. No chmod 777 used.
